title: Game News Round-Up
summary: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 26 June 2026.

items.title: Microsoft is raising the price of **Xbox** consoles due to the ongoing hardware shortages
bullets: Series X 2TB Galaxy Black is being discontinued
The price of XBOX consoles will increase by $100 for 512 GB models and $150 for 1 TB models
Microsoft have confirmed the prices will be updated worldwide, not just in the US

items.title: **Sony** confirms it’s laying off ‘most of the Destiny team’, ‘some’ Marathon devs  as well as members across SIE teams that 'support Bungie's operations'
bullets: It is believed that Bungie studio head Justin Truman, who succeeded Pete Parsons last year, is stepping down following the news of layoffs

items.title: At least a dozen people have departed award-winning South of Midnight developer **Compulsion Games** this week, following reports that Xbox is planning major restructuring that could result in "significant" layoffs and studio closures
bullets: LinkedIn posts by a dozen former Compulsion staff, shared during the past week, highlight a string of departures, with at least one former employee "unexpectedly" finding themselves without a job and now looking for work

items.title: VC fund **VGames** launches new $10m Indie Fund. The venture is providing project financing in exchange for revenue share rather than equity deals
San Francisco-based video game investment house **Denmu** announces 'auteur-first' $50 million fund for game devs
